m_Item1.QuestionText: Which Namespace is used to "Display" in Data Annotation using MVC ?
m_Item1.Explanation: System.ComponentModel.DataAnnotations: This namespace in .NET provides various classes and attributes for validation, displaying data, and defining metadata for entities in Entity Framework and ASP.NET MVC applications. Specifically, the DisplayAttribute is part of this namespace and is used to specify how data fields are displayed and labeled in views generated by ASP.NET MVC.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is True?

Options.OptionText: Entity Framework is an open source ORM framework.
Entity Framework is Sql Server database mapping tool.
Entity Framework is object mapping tool using which we can not insert, update and delete data.

m_Item1.Explanation: Entity Framework is an open-source ORM framework for . NET applications supported by Microsoft. It enables developers to work with data using objects of domain specific classes without focusing on the underlying database tables and columns where this data is stored.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
A pattern of loading related data where a query for one type of entity also loads related entities as part of
the query is called:
m_Item1.Explanation: Eager loading is the process whereby a query for one type of entity also loads related entities as part of the query.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following development approaches are supported in Entity Framework?
m_Item1.Explanation:
Entity Framework (EF) supports multiple development approaches to designing and working with databases:

  • Code First: This approach allows you to define your data model using C# or VB.NET classes. The database schema is then automatically generated based on these classes when the application runs.

  • Database First: With this approach, you start with an existing database schema. EF generates the corresponding C# or VB.NET classes (entities) based on the database schema.

  • Model First: In this approach, you use the EF Designer in Visual Studio to visually design the data model (entities and relationships). The database schema and classes are then generated from this conceptual model.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What window in Visual Studio display CSDL, MSL and SSDL of Entity Framework?
m_Item1.Explanation: EDM Designer (Entity Data Model Designer): This window in Visual Studio is specifically designed for Entity Framework and displays the CSDL (Conceptual Schema Definition Language), MSL (Mapping Specification Language), and SSDL (Store Schema Definition Language). It allows developers to visually design and manipulate the entity data model, including defining entities, their relationships, and how they map to the underlying database schema.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is TRUE?

Options.OptionText: DbContext can not be used in Code First approach
ObjectContext is a wrapper around DBContext
DbContext is a wrapper around ObjectContext
DbContext is a sealed class which cannot be override.

m_Item1.Explanation: DBContext is a wrapper of ObjectContext that exposes the most commonly used features of ObjectContext. In contrast, Object Context is a class of the core Entity framework API that allows performing queries and tracking the updates made to a database using strongly typed entity classes.

m_Item1.QuestionText: CSDL stands for
m_Item1.Explanation: CSDL stands for Conceptual Schema Definition Language. It is part of the Entity Framework and represents the conceptual model of entities, their relationships, and properties within the application. CSDL defines the structure and conceptual view of the data model independent of the underlying database schema.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following query syntax can be used to query EDM?
m_Item1.Explanation:
You can build and execute queries using Entity Framework to fetch the data from the underlying database. EF 6 supports different types of queries which in turn convert into SQL queries for the underlying database.

Entity framework supports three types of queries: 1) LINQ-to-Entities, 2) Entity SQL, and 3) Native SQL

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following approaches in Entity Framework allows you to define your data model using C# or VB.NET classes and generates the database schema automatically when the application runs?
m_Item1.Explanation: This approach allows developers to define the data model using POCO (Plain Old CLR Object) classes. The database schema is generated automatically based on these classes when the application runs. This approach is beneficial for developers who prefer to focus on code rather than database design upfront.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is NOT a type of entity?
m_Item1.Explanation: DBSet: In Entity Framework, DBSet represents a collection of entities of a specific type within a context. It is not a type of entity itself but rather a DbSet<TEntity> used to query and interact with entities of type TEntity within a database context.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is NOT TRUE about the Entity Framework?

Options.OptionText:
It automatically generates the classes from the model and updates these classes dynamically when the
model is changed.
It takes care of database connectivity.
It provides query syntax for querying the model
It does not provide any mechanism to track changes to the model’s objects.

m_Item1.Explanation: It does not provide any mechanism to track changes to the model’s objects: This statement is false. Entity Framework includes change tracking mechanisms that automatically track changes to entities (objects) within a context. This allows EF to manage updates, inserts, and deletes when saving changes back to the database.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is responsible for change tracking management?
m_Item1.Explanation: ObjectStateManager: In Entity Framework, the ObjectStateManager is responsible for change tracking management. It tracks changes to entities (objects) within a DbContext or ObjectContext, including additions, modifications, and deletions. This tracking enables Entity Framework to efficiently manage and persist changes back to the database when SaveChanges() is called.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How to disable Lazy loading using DBContext?
m_Item1.Explanation: Lazy loading in Entity Framework allows related entities to be automatically loaded from the database when accessed. Disabling lazy loading can be useful in scenarios where you want to explicitly control when related entities are loaded to avoid performance issues like N+1 queries.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which interface you have to implement to get the reference of ObjectContext from DBContext?
m_Item1.Explanation: IObjectContextAdapter: This interface is used to access the underlying ObjectContext from a DbContext in Entity Framework. When working with Entity Framework, especially in scenarios where you need to access lower-level features or APIs of ObjectContext that are not directly exposed by DbContext, implementing IObjectContextAdapter allows you to get a reference to the underlying ObjectContext.

m_Item1.QuestionText: An API that can be used to configure a Code First model is called:
m_Item1.Explanation: Fluent API: In Entity Framework Code First approach, the Fluent API provides a way to configure the model using fluent syntax in C#. It allows developers to define entity configurations, relationships, keys, indexes, etc., using methods and lambda expressions, providing more control and flexibility over the configuration compared to data annotations.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In EF Core, what is a DbContext?

Options.OptionText: A class representing the database schema.
A class responsible for defining database queries.
A class representing a database connection.
A class responsible for interacting with the database and mapping entities.

m_Item1.Explanation: In EF Core, a DbContext is a class responsible for interacting with the database and mapping entities to database tables. It represents a session with the database and provides an API to query, insert, update, and delete data.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What tool is used to perform migrations in Entity Framework Core?
m_Item1.Explanation:
The 'dotnet ef' command is a tool for Entity Framework Core that is used to add, remove, or apply migrations.
What is dotnet ef used for?
Creating migrations (dotnet ef migrations add <Name>)
Applying migrations (dotnet ef database update)
Removing migrations (dotnet ef migrations remove)
Viewing database schema (dotnet ef dbcontext info)

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is Entity Framework in .NET?
m_Item1.Explanation: Entity Framework is an object-relational mapping (ORM) framework for .NET applications, allowing developers to work with databases using .NET objects.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is NOT a workflow supported by Entity Framework?
m_Item1.Explanation: Entity Framework supports Code-First, Model-First, and Database-First workflows. The Client-First workflow does not exist in Entity Framework.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which command is used to add a migration in Entity Framework using the Package Manager Console in Visual Studio?
m_Item1.Explanation: Add-Migration is the command used to add a new migration in Entity Framework when using the Package Manager Console.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In a Code-First approach, which class is responsible for defining the database context?
m_Item1.Explanation: The DbContext class is responsible for interacting with the database in a Code-First approach and is used to define the database context.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of DbSet in Entity Framework?

Options.OptionText: To interact with the file system

m_Item1.Explanation: DbSet is used in Entity Framework to represent collections of entities from a database and essentially corresponds to tables.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following best describes 'lazy loading' in Entity Framework?

Options.OptionText: Loading related data explicitly using a Load method
Loading all related data at once with the initial query
Deferring data loading until the data is accessed

m_Item1.Explanation: Lazy loading in Entity Framework defers the loading of related data until it is specifically accessed.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of the 'OnModelCreating' method in Entity Framework?
m_Item1.Explanation: The OnModelCreating method is used to configure the schema of the model by overriding it in the DbContext class.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How does Entity Framework ensure data integrity between related tables?
m_Item1.Explanation: Entity Framework uses foreign keys to ensure data integrity between related tables, maintaining the relationships defined in the model.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is 'eager loading' in the context of Entity Framework?

Options.OptionText: Loading related entities as part of the initial query
Loading data on a separate thread
Skipping data loading until an entity is saved
Loading only primary data, deferring the rest

m_Item1.Explanation: Eager loading is a process in Entity Framework where related entities are loaded as part of the initial query to avoid additional querying.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method is used to execute raw SQL queries in Entity Framework?
m_Item1.Explanation: The FromSqlRaw method is used in Entity Framework Core to execute raw SQL queries and map the results to a defined entity type.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary benefit of using Code First approach in Entity Framework?

Options.OptionText: It allows you to generate a database from an existing model.
It makes it easy to manage database schema changes using code.
It allows you to use SQL queries directly within C# code.
It provides a graphical interface for managing database tables.

m_Item1.Explanation: The Code First approach allows you to define your model using C# classes and then generate the database schema from these classes, making it easier to manage changes in the database by updating the code.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following methods is used to apply migrations in Code First approach?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'Update-Database' command in the Package Manager Console is used to apply migrations to the database in the Code First approach.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In Code First approach, how do you configure the primary key of a model class?

Options.OptionText: By defining a primary key constraint in the database manually.

m_Item1.Explanation: You can configure the primary key in Code First by using the '[Key]' data annotation on the property you want to designate as the primary key.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How does Code First approach handle relationships between classes?

Options.OptionText: Automatically assumes all properties are unrelated.
Requires explicit configuration of each relationship using Fluent API.
Uses navigation properties and data annotations to determine relationships.

m_Item1.Explanation: Code First can determine relationships using navigation properties and data annotations, but you can also use the Fluent API for more advanced configurations.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which feature of Code First enables you to maintain database schema without losing existing data?
m_Item1.Explanation: Migrations in Code First allow you to incrementally update the database schema without losing existing data, providing a way to evolve your database as your model changes.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the first step in creating a Database First model using Entity Framework in a .NET application?

Options.OptionText: Define all classes and properties in the code first.
Use Entity Data Model Wizard to import the existing database schema.
Manually write SQL scripts to generate database tables.
Use Visual Studio to create an empty database project.

m_Item1.Explanation: In the Database First approach, the first step is to use the Entity Data Model Wizard to import the existing database schema into the application and generate the corresponding model classes.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which file extension is associated with the Entity Data Model created using Database First approach in Entity Framework?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .edmx file is the XML file used to define the Entity Data Model when using the Database First approach with the Entity Framework.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How are changes in the database schema reflected in the Entity Data Model when using the Database First approach?

Options.OptionText: By manually editing the code files generated by Entity Framework.
By running the entity framework migrations.
By updating the .edmx model through the 'Update Model from Database' option in Visual Studio.
By rebuilding the entire project which automatically syncs the database changes.

m_Item1.Explanation: In the Database First approach, you reflect changes in the database schema by updating the .edmx model using the 'Update Model from Database' option in Visual Studio.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following best describes the role of the T4 template in the Database First approach?

Options.OptionText: It generates the entire SQL for the initial database setup.
It creates custom extensions for Entity Framework.
It automates the creation of strongly typed classes and contexts based on the .edmx file.
It facilitates the integration with third-party databases.

m_Item1.Explanation: T4 templates are used to automate the generation of strongly typed context and entity classes from the .edmx model, which eases development with the Entity Framework.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Why might a developer choose the Database First approach over the Code First approach in Entity Framework?

Options.OptionText: The Code First approach is only compatible with SQL Server databases.
Database First offers more control over the generated SQL queries.
Database First is suited for projects that start with an existing, complex database schema.
The Code First approach doesn't support migrations in Entity Framework.

m_Item1.Explanation: Database First is ideal for projects where the database schema is already established and possibly complex, as it allows the project to be built around the existing database structure.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following data annotation in Entity Framework is used to specify that a property is required?
m_Item1.Explanation: The [Required] data annotation is used to indicate that a property should not be null and a value is required.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of the [StringLength] data annotation in Entity Framework?

Options.OptionText: To ensure the property is unique
To specify minimum and maximum length for a string property
To make a property a primary key
To change the column type in the database

m_Item1.Explanation: [StringLength] sets the maximum, and optionally the minimum, number of characters for a column. It is used for validation and to create an appropriate column size in the database.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which data annotation is specifically used to designate a primary key in a model?
m_Item1.Explanation: The [Key] data annotation explicitly marks a property as the primary key of the entity.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How does the [ConcurrencyCheck] data annotation function in Entity Framework?

Options.OptionText: It indicates a property as a candidate key
It marks a property to be part of the concurrency token used in update operations
It sets the column type to VARCHAR
It maps a property to a specific database field

m_Item1.Explanation: The [ConcurrencyCheck] attribute is used to indicate that a property should be checked for concurrency conflicts when the data is updated.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which annotation allows you to map a property to a specific database column by name in Entity Framework?
m_Item1.Explanation: The [Column] annotation allows customization of the column that a property is mapped to, such as specifying a column name, order, and data type.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which interface is commonly implemented to perform custom validation in Entity Framework?
m_Item1.Explanation: In Entity Framework, the IValidatableObject interface is used to perform custom validation logic on the server side. Implementing this interface allows developers to define a custom Validate method for complex validation.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What attribute is used to enforce a property as required in an entity class?
m_Item1.Explanation: The [Required] attribute is used in Entity Framework to ensure that a property must contain a value. This is useful for validation purposes to prevent null entries in the database for that field.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method is typically overridden to include validation logic in Entity Framework's DbContext?
m_Item1.Explanation: ValidateEntity is a method that can be overridden in the DbContext class to include custom validation logic when entities are being saved. It allows developers to inject validation logic centrally.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which data annotation attribute would you use to specify a string length for a property in Entity Framework?
m_Item1.Explanation: The [StringLength] attribute is used to specify the maximum length of a string property, which is a common validation requirement in Entity Framework for ensuring that data does not exceed a specified length limit.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you execute validation logic before saving changes in Entity Framework using Fluent API?
m_Item1.Explanation: In Fluent API, validation logic can be included in the OnModelCreating() method. This method allows for the configuration of models and relationships, as well as the application of custom validation rules.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of a primary key in Entity Framework?

Options.OptionText: To ensure each entity instance is unique
To define complex types in the model

m_Item1.Explanation: A primary key uniquely identifies each entity instance within a database, ensuring that no two instances have the same primary key value.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What happens if you define a primary key as nullable in Entity Framework?

Options.OptionText: EF automatically handles null primary keys by assigning default values
The entity cannot be tracked by the change tracker
It allows duplicate primary key values

m_Item1.Explanation: A primary key must always have a value, as it uniquely identifies a record. Defining it as nullable will cause a runtime or configuration error.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In Entity Framework, what attribute is commonly used to specify a property as a primary key?
m_Item1.Explanation: The [Key] attribute is used to denote a property as the primary key for an entity in Entity Framework.

m_Item1.QuestionText: If an entity in Entity Framework does not have an explicitly defined primary key, what will Entity Framework do?

Options.OptionText: Entity Framework will automatically create a composite key using all properties
Entity Framework will throw a runtime exception
Entity Framework will try to infer the primary key based on naming conventions
Entity Framework does not require a primary key and will proceed with other configurations

m_Item1.Explanation: Entity Framework uses conventions to infer which property is the primary key, typically a property named 'Id' or a combination of the class name and 'Id'.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following scenarios requires explicitly defining a composite primary key in Entity Framework?

Options.OptionText: When an entity has only one unique attribute
When the primary key spans multiple columns
When using a GUID as the primary key

m_Item1.Explanation: A composite primary key is necessary when the primary key consists of multiple columns, which must be explicitly defined in Entity Framework.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is a foreign key in the context of Entity Framework?

Options.OptionText: A primary key that is unique across multiple tables.
An index that improves query performance across tables.
A key used to reference the primary key in another table.
A view created to combine data from different tables.

m_Item1.Explanation: In Entity Framework, a foreign key is used to establish a relationship between two tables by referencing the primary key of another table.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When configuring a foreign key in Entity Framework Code First, what attribute can you use to specify the foreign key?
m_Item1.Explanation: The [ForeignKey] attribute is used to specify which property should act as the foreign key in a relationship.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In which scenario would using the Fluent API be more appropriate than Data Annotations for configuring a foreign key in Entity Framework?

Options.OptionText: When you want an entity to have no foreign key relationships.
When configurations are complex and not easily handled by annotations.
When defining a primary key for the entity.

m_Item1.Explanation: The Fluent API offers more granular control over model configuration, which is suitable for complex configurations that go beyond what Data Annotations can provide.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How does Entity Framework handle foreign keys by default when using Code First migrations?

Options.OptionText: It requires explicit mapping for all foreign keys.
It automatically infers foreign key relationships from navigation properties.
It disallows foreign key creation in Code First approach.

m_Item1.Explanation: Entity Framework automatically infers foreign key relationships when it detects navigation properties between classes in a model.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method in Entity Framework Core's Fluent API is primarily used to configure foreign key relationships?
m_Item1.Explanation: The HasForeignKey method is used in Entity Framework Core's Fluent API to define which property in an entity should be used as a foreign key.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is a Fluent API in Entity Framework primarily used for?

Options.OptionText: Generating HTML views in an MVC application
Configuring the model using LINQ queries
Programmatically configuring the model and its relationships

m_Item1.Explanation: Fluent API is used in Entity Framework to configure the model's properties and relationships programmatically using code rather than data annotations or conventions.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method is typically overridden to use Fluent API for model configuration?
m_Item1.Explanation: The OnModelCreating method is overridden in the DbContext class to configure the model using Fluent API.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a valid use of Fluent API to set a composite key?
m_Item1.Explanation: Fluent API allows setting composite keys using the HasKey method by specifying multiple properties in an anonymous object.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which Fluent API method would you use to specify that a property is required?
m_Item1.Explanation:
The IsRequired method is used in Fluent API to specify that a property is required, meaning it cannot be null.
it's part of the Fluent API, not Data Annotations

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of the Fluent API method HasMaxLength when applied to a string property?

Options.OptionText: To specify the maximum number of characters allowed for the string property
To set the default value of the string property
To make the string property unique
To change the data type of the string property

m_Item1.Explanation: The HasMaxLength method is used to specify the maximum number of characters allowed for a string property, restricting its size in the database.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary purpose of database migrations in Entity Framework?

Options.OptionText: To manage and apply changes to the database schema.
To optimize database performance by indexing.

m_Item1.Explanation: Database migrations in Entity Framework are primarily used to manage and apply changes to the database schema. They provide a way to evolve the database schema over time as the application requirements change.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which command is used to create a new migration in Entity Framework Core?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'Add-Migration' command is used to create a new migration in Entity Framework Core. It generates a new migration file with the changes detected in the model compared to the current database schema.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the role of the 'Up' method in a migration class in Entity Framework?

Options.OptionText: To rollback changes made by the migration.
To define the logic that applies the migration.
To create a snapshot of the database state.

m_Item1.Explanation: The 'Up' method in a migration class defines the logic required to apply the changes to the database schema, such as creating tables, adding columns, or modifying constraints.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you revert the last migration in Entity Framework Core?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'Remove-Migration' command in Entity Framework Core is used to revert the last migration. It rolls back the changes made by the most recent migration locally and does not affect the database.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which file holds the list of applied migrations in an Entity Framework Core project?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'dbo.__EFMigrationsHistory' table in the database holds the list of applied migrations in an Entity Framework Core project. This table tracks which migrations have been applied to the database.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does CRUD stand for in the context of Entity Framework?
m_Item1.Explanation: CRUD is an acronym for Create, Read, Update, and Delete - the four fundamental operations of persistent storage in database management.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method in Entity Framework is used to update an existing record in the database?
m_Item1.Explanation: The context.SaveChanges() method is used to persist changes to the database, including updating modified records.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which statement will you use to delete an entity in Entity Framework?
m_Item1.Explanation: In Entity Framework, the Remove method is used to delete an entity from the context. Once SaveChanges() is called, the entity is deleted from the database.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When performing a read operation in Entity Framework, which method is used to retrieve all records from a table?
m_Item1.Explanation: The ToList() method is used to retrieve all records from a table in Entity Framework by converting the DbSet to a List.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In Entity Framework, what is the correct way to add a new entity to the context?
m_Item1.Explanation: The Add() method is used to add a new entity to the context in Entity Framework. The changes are saved to the database with SaveChanges().

m_Item1.QuestionText: How does Entity Framework handle concurrency conflicts during update operations?
m_Item1.Explanation: Entity Framework uses optimistic concurrency control, where it checks if the data has been modified since it was loaded. If there is a conflict, an exception is thrown.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following methods in Entity Framework is used to track changes made to an entity before calling SaveChanges()?
m_Item1.Explanation: The DetectChanges() method detects changes made to the tracked entities and updates their states accordingly.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the default isolation level used by Entity Framework when performing transactions?
m_Item1.Explanation: Entity Framework uses the 'Read Committed' isolation level by default, which ensures that any data read is committed at the moment it is read.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which approach does Entity Framework use for loading related data by default?
m_Item1.Explanation: By default, Entity Framework uses lazy loading to load related data. This means related data is loaded automatically when it is accessed for the first time.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is the recommended way to perform a batch update in Entity Framework?

Options.OptionText: Iterating over each item and calling SaveChanges() inside the loop
Directly manipulating the database using ADO.NET

m_Item1.Explanation: The ExecuteSqlCommand method allows for executing raw SQL commands for batch updates, which is more efficient than updating and saving entities individually.
